title: prunus × hillieri
intro: Prunus × hillieri, also known as Hillier's cherry, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Prunus avium (wild cherry) and Prunus fruticosa (manchurian cherry). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains prunasin, a cyanogenic glycoside that has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Prunus × hillieri include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Prunus × hillieri can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, including decoction, infusion, and tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
